channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Court-martial convenes for Pentagon leaker already facing years behind bars
item.description: BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A court-martial convened on Monday for Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, who was sentenced in federal court last year to 15 years in prison for leaking highly classified military documents after the most consequential national security breach in years.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Para climber reaches new heights after injury, wants others to join her
item.description: "The battle's not against my competitors, like the battle is about how well I do," said Nat Vorel, a paraclimber.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Ogle/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Fishermen pull 'wrapped' body out of water off coast of Massachusetts
item.description: The crew of the boat had reported finding the body in its fishing nets about 40 miles from the Massachusetts coast on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KREX 5

item.title: Arizona prisoner set to be executed next week passes up chance to ask for reprieve
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A prisoner scheduled to be executed next week in what would be Arizona’s first use of the death penalty in over two years has passed up a chance to ask for a reprieve from his death sentence.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Study says climate change will even make Earth's orbit a mess
item.description: A new study finds that climate change is already causing all sorts of problems on Earth, but soon it will be making a mess in orbit around the planet too.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio confirms ICE arrest of pro-Palestinian Columbia student protest leader
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has confirmed the arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a green card-holding Columbia University graduate student and pro-Palestinian activist, by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Check out Google's new Santa Monica store
item.description: Apple has over 500 retail stores worldwide, but Google is taking a more selective approach, opening physical stores in key locations. Recently, I attended the grand opening of the company's sixth location, which is now officially open in Santa Monica at the Third Street Promenade.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: South Carolina man called for end of death penalty before execution in final statement
item.description: "I want my closing statement to be one of love and a calling to my fellow Christians to help us end the death penalty," Brad Kieth Sigmon wrote ahead of his execution.
item.dc:creator: Kelci O'Donnell, ABC 4

item.title: Robert Pattison sci-fi ‘Mickey 17’ opens in first place, but profitability is a long way off
item.description: “Parasite” filmmaker Bong Joon Ho’s original science fiction film “Mickey 17” opened in first place on the North American box office charts.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: CDC announces probe into possible link between autism, vaccines
item.description: The CDC conducted previous investigations, confirming past scientific studies that found no link between the two.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Do you need fluoride after a teeth cleaning? Dental experts weigh in
item.description: Dentists and hygienists often propose fluoride treatments to patients in the dental chair to prevent cavities and strengthen teeth. But the relatively simple procedure – which involves applying a varnish, gel or foam directly to the teeth – isn’t always covered by insurance, especially for adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fire risk remains high on Long Island after brush fires
item.description: The fire danger remains high on Long Island as officials work to quell wind-driven brush fires that broke out Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KRON4

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: An early version of the Egg McMuffin included a surprising condiment
item.description: For a brief time, McDonald's envisioned their now-signature breakfast sandwich with a sweet component.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rat population increases linked to climate change: Study
item.description: Using rat and inspection data from 16 major cities between 2007 and 2024, researchers found that Washington, D.C. gained more rats than others studied.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Ghost preparers and spear phishing: Here's the tax scams to avoid
item.description: With the tax filing deadline coming up on April 15, the IRS released its annual "Dirty Dozen" list of tax scams for 2025 to warn people of common schemes.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Secret Service shoots man in overnight 'armed confrontation' near White House
item.description: The Secret Service shot a man during an armed confrontation about a block away the White House just after midnight Sunday, the agency announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hateful message left on restaurant receipt results in outpouring of support, staff says
item.description: The public is rallying behind a central Ohio Mexican restaurant after a customer left a hateful message instead of a tip.
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman, KTLA 5

item.title: Warriors star Stephen Curry reaches 25,000 career points
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Stephen Curry became the 26th player in NBA history to score 25,000 career points, reaching the milestone during the third quarter of the Golden State Warriors' 115-110 victory over the Detroit Pistons on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New York governor declares state of emergency for wind-driven brush fire on Long Island
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fast-moving brush fires fanned by high winds burned through a large swath of land on New York's Long Island on Saturday, spewing thick gray smoke into the sky and prompting the evacuation of a military base and the closure of a major highway. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 days of clashes and revenge killings in Syria leave more than 1,000 people dead
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The death toll from two days of clashes between Syrian security forces and loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad and revenge killings that followed has risen to more than 1,000, a war monitoring group said Saturday, making it one of the deadliest acts of violence since Syria’s con
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 8 hurt when driver plows vehicle into Southern California car dealership
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Eight people were injured Saturday when a driver plowed a vehicle into a CarMax location in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, KTLA 5

item.title: Alabama minor is jailed with adults pre-trial in violation of federal law
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Djovani Jean-Pierre spent his 17th birthday in a small county jail in rural northern Alabama, where he has languished for more than half the school year awaiting trial. On the advice of his 51-year-old cellmate, Jean-Pierre avoids contact with others in the jail.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police: Colorado woman with 7 kids pointed gun at another driver in road rage incident
item.description: A woman was arrested after she allegedly pointed a gun at another driver in Commerce City on Thursday while seven kids were in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Williams, Fox 5

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Raiders acquire quarterback Geno Smith from Seahawks for a 3rd-round pick, AP source says
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders answered their most pressing personnel question of the offseason Friday by acquiring quarterback Geno Smith in a trade with Seattle and reuniting him with his former coach, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Federal judge won't order immigration officials to change school arrests policy
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Immigration authorities don't need to revert to a Biden-era policy limiting arrests at schools after officials in Denver challenged new policies from the Trump administration, a federal judge ruled Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawsuit aims to strike down LGBTQ antidiscrimination protections in Pennsylvania
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Two public school districts and several parents have sued Pennsylvania in a bid to undo antidiscrimination protections for gay and transgender people, saying the state's two-year-old regulation is illegal because it goes beyond what lawmakers intended or allowed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change and habitat loss, experts said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: Trump, who once called crypto a "scam," has drastically changed his tune on the subject and promised on the campaign trail to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9
